scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE PRINCIPAL CITY CIVIL JUDGE, BENGALURU

ORIGINAL SUIT NO. _____ OF 2024

Ram Kumar,
S/o Late Shri Krishnan Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
R/o No. 42, 4th Cross, Jayanagar,
Bengaluru, Karnataka –

560070.                                                    ... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
S/o Shri Ramesh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
R/o No. 78, 3rd Block, Koramangala,
Bengaluru, Karnataka –

560034.                                                    ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY

The Plaintiff above named most respectfully submits as follows:

I. PRELIMINARY

1. The Plaintiff is a law-abiding citizen residing at Jayanagar, Bengaluru, and is engaged in business activities. The Plaintiff is entitled to sue for recovery of the money lent to the Defendant.

2. The Defendant is a resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, and is a known acquaintance of the Plaintiff. The Defendant is liable to be sued in this Hon'ble Court having jurisdiction over the area where the Defendant resides and carries on business.

3. The subject matter of the suit being recovery of a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) together with interest, this Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try and determine the present suit.

II. FACTS OF THE CASE

4. The Plaintiff and the Defendant are known to each other. The Defendant approached the Plaintiff in March 2024 requesting a hand loan for his personal and business requirements.

5. Relying on the oral request and assurance of the Defendant to repay the loan within a short period, the Plaintiff agreed to advance a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the Defendant as a friendly hand loan.

6. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff transferred a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the bank account of the Defendant through NEFT banking transaction, the UTR number being AXIB20240315123456.

7. There was no written agreement executed between the parties regarding the said loan transaction. However, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the loan amount and promised to repay the same.

8. The Defendant acknowledged the loan through WhatsApp messages exchanged between the parties, wherein the Defendant admitted the borrowing and assured repayment.

9. The Defendant failed to repay the loan amount within the time promised. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands to the Defendant for repayment of the loan amount.

10. Despite the oral demands made by the Plaintiff, the Defendant failed and neglected to repay the loan amount.

11. The date of default for the purpose of interest calculation is 20.06.2024, from which date the Defendant failed to repay the amount as promised.

12. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the Defendant calling upon him to repay the loan amount along with interest.

13. The Defendant has failed to comply with the legal notice dated 01.07.2024 and has not repaid the loan amount.

14. The Defendant is liable to pay the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- together with interest at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of default, i.e., 20.06.2024, until realization.

III. STATEMENT OF ACCOUNT

15. The Statement of Account showing the amount due and payable by the Defendant to the Plaintiff is as under:

| Sl. No. | Particulars | Amount (Rs.) |

|---------|-------------|-------------|

|

16. | Principal amount advanced on 15.03.2024 | 15,00,000/- |

|

17. | Part-payments received | NIL |

|

18. | Balance principal amount due | 15,00,000/- |

|

19. | Interest @ 12% p.a. from 20.06.2024 to date of filing | As applicable |

|

20. | Total amount claimed | 15,00,000/- plus interest |

IV. LEGAL BASIS

21. Under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, when a contract is broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation from the party who has broken the contract for any loss or damage caused to him thereby. The Defendant's failure to repay the loan amount constitutes a breach of the contract, entitling the Plaintiff to compensation.

22. Under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, the Civil Court has jurisdiction to try all suits of a civil nature excepting suits of which the cognizance is either expressly or impliedly barred. The present suit being of civil nature for recovery of money, this Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to try the same.

23. Under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, every suit shall be instituted in the Court within the local limits of whose jurisdiction the Defendant resides or carries on business. The Defendant resides within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court, and hence this suit is instituted before this Hon'ble Court.

24. Under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, the Court may award interest at such rate as the Court deems reasonable on the principal sum adjudged from the date of the suit to the date of payment. The Plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum as reasonable compensation for be
